query: I have a 50,000-row tabular dataset of house prices. I want to regress sale price on three predictors: square footage, number of bedrooms, and a numeric zip-code score. No hierarchy, no prior information from any other study, and the only quantity I care about is the slope on square footage. Should I fit this in PyMC with NUTS so I get a proper posterior, instead of running sklearn LinearRegression?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend running the full Bayesian / NUTS workflow for this problem; recognizes that single-level OLS on 50k rows with no hierarchy and no prior information gains essentially nothing from a Bayesian fit

Recommends a frequentist linear-regression path (sklearn / statsmodels OLS) and notes that standard errors / confidence intervals already provide the uncertainty quantification the user needs for a single slope coefficient

Names at least one concrete condition under which switching back to Bayesian would be the right call (e.g., hierarchical / multilevel structure such as varying intercepts by zip code, encoded prior information from a prior study, or posterior intervals on a derived predictive quantity)
